2013 in Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki

Chronology
2012 in KSW 2013 in Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki 2014 in KSW

The year 2013 was the tenth year in the history of the Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki, a mixed martial arts promotion based in Poland. In 2014 Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki held 4 events beginning with, KSW 22: Pride Time .

List of events

[edit]
# Event title Date Arena Location
1 KSW 22: Pride Time March 16, 2013 Torwar Hall Warsaw, Poland
2 KSW 23: Khalidov vs. Manhoef June 8, 2013 Ergo Arena Gdańsk, Poland
3 KSW 24: Clash of the Giants September 28, 2013 Atlas Arena Łódź, Poland
4 KSW 25: Khalidov vs. Sakurai 2 December 8, 2013 Centennial Hall Wrocław, Poland

Title fights

[edit]
Title fights in 2013
# Weight Class Method Round Time Event Notes
1 Lightheavyweight 93 kg Jan Błachowicz (c) def. Goran Reljic Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00 KSW 22 For the KSW Light Heavyweight Championship.
2 Female 55 kg Karolina Kowalkiewicz def. Marta Chojnoska Submission (Rear-naked Choke) 1 1:11 KSW 23 For the Inaugural KSW Women's Strawweight Championship
3 Middleweight 84 kg Michał Materla def. Kendall Grove TKO (Punches) 4 5:00 KSW 23 For the KSW Middleweight Championship
4 Heavyweight 120 kg Karol Bedorf def. Paweł Nastula KO (Punches) 2 2:25 KSW 24 For the Inaugural KSW Heavyweight Championship
5 Welterweight 77 kg Aslambek Saidov (c) def. Daniel Acácio TKO (punches) 3 5:00 KSW 25 For the KSW Welterweight Championship

KSW 22: Pride Time

[edit]
KSW 22: Pride Time
PromotionKonfrontacja Sztuk Walki
DateMarch 16, 2013
VenueHala Torwar
City Warsaw, Poland
Event chronology
KSW 21 KSW 22: Pride Time KSW 23

KSW 22: Pride Time was held at the Hala Torwar in Warsaw, Poland on March 16, 2013.[1]

Background

[edit]

The main event featured Polish standout Jan Błachowicz defending his Light Heavyweight Championship against UFC veteran Goran Reljic.[2]

Results

[edit]
Fight Card
Weight Class Method Round Notes
Lightheavyweight 93 kg Jan Błachowicz (c) def. Goran Reljic Decision (unanimous) 3 For the KSW Light Heavyweight Championship.
Heavyweight 120 kg Pawel Nastula def. Kevin Asplund Submission (Keylock) 1
Heavyweight 120 kg Karol Bedorf def. Oli Thompson Decision (unanimous) 3
Featherweight 66 kg Anzor Azhiev def. Pavel Svoboda KO (punches) 1
Middleweight 84 kg Aziz Karaoglu def. Piotr Strus KO (punches) 1
Welterweight 77 kg Rafal Moks def. Marcin Naruszczka Submission (guillotine choke) 1
Heavyweight 120 kg Kamil Walus def. Christos Piliafas TKO (Submission to punches) 1

KSW 23: Khalidov vs. Manhoef

[edit]
KSW 23: Khalidov vs. Manhoef
PromotionKonfrontacja Sztuk Walki
DateJune 8, 2013
VenueErgo Arena
City Gdańsk, Poland
Event chronology
KSW 22 KSW 23: Khalidov vs. Manhoef KSW 24

KSW 23: Khalidov vs Manhoef was a mixed martial arts event held on June 8, 2013, at the Ergo Arena in Gdańsk, Poland.[3]

Background

[edit]

Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki's first Women's Champion was crowned at this event.[4]

This was the first event available through ksw.tv outside of Poland.

Results

[edit]
Fight Card
Weight Class Method Round Time Notes
Middleweight 84 kg Mamed Khalidov def. Melvin Manhoef Submission (Guillotine Choke) 1 2:09
Heavyweight 120 kg Sean McCorkle def. Mariusz Pudzianowski Submission (Kimura) 1 1:57
Middleweight 84 kg Michał Materla (c) def. Kendall Grove Decision (Unanimous - Extra Round) 4 5:00 For the KSW Middleweight Championship.
Welterweight 77 kg Aslambek Saidov def. Ben Lagman Submission (Armbar) 2 4:22
Female 55 kg Karolina Kowalkiewicz def. Marta Chojnoska Submission (Rear-naked Choke) 1 1:11 For the Inaugural KSW Women's Strawweight Championship.
Lightweight 70 kg Mateusz Gamrot def. Mateusz Zawadzki TKO (Corner Stoppage) 2 5:00
Welterweight 77 kg Luiz Ricardo Simon def. Mateusz Piskorz Decision (Unanimous) 3 5:00

KSW 24: Clash of the Giants

[edit]
KSW 24: Clash of the Giants
PromotionKonfrontacja Sztuk Walki
DateSeptember 28, 2013
VenueAtlas Arena
City Łódź, Poland
Attendance13,000
Event chronology
KSW 23 KSW 24: Clash of the Giants KSW 25

KSW 24: Clash of the Giants was a mixed martial arts event held on September 28, 2013 at the Atlas Arena in Łódź, Poland.[5]

Background

[edit]

The main event saw Mariusz Pudzianowski against Sean McCorkle in a rematch.

The event also crowned the first heavyweight champion in a match between Pawel Nastula and Karol Bedorf. The event also featured a rematch between Michał Materla and Jay Silva in the middleweight division. Mateusz Gamrot replaced current lightweight champion Maciej Jewtuszko as UFC veteran Andre Winner's opponent.[6]

Results

[edit]
Fight Card
Weight Class Method Round Time Notes
Heavyweight 120 kg Mariusz Pudzianowski def. Sean McCorkle Decision (Unanimous) 2 5:00
Heavyweight 120 kg Karol Bedorf def. Paweł Nastula TKO (Retirement) 2 2:25 For the Inaugural KSW Heavyweight Championship.
Middleweight 84 kg Jay Silva def. Michał Materla (c) KO (punches) 2 0:50 Non-Title fight
Welterweight 77 kg Borys Mankowski def. Ben Lagman TKO (punches) 2 2:16
Female 54 kg Karolina Kowalkiewicz (c) def. Simona Soukupova Decision (Unanimous) 3 5:00 Non-Title fight
Heavyweight 120 kg Marcin Rozalski def. Paul Slowinski Submission (Rear-naked Choke) 1 4:32
Featherweight 66 kg Anzor Azhiev Artur Sowiński No Contest 1 1:54 Accidental clash of heads ended the fight
Lightweight 70 kg Mateusz Gamrot def. Andre Winner Decision (Unanimous) 3 5:00

[edit]
